heading: Definitions
label: 561 Definitions
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior , title XVI, , , amended sections 114 and 40119 of Title 49, Transportation, see , set out as a note below.section 1601 of Pub. L. 107–296116 Stat. 2312section 3(c) of Pub. L. 113–245Nov. 25, 2002

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–245, § 2128 Stat. 2871Dec. 18, 2014“Congress finds the following:“(1)The Transportation Security Administration has not consistently implemented Department of Homeland Security policies and Government best practices for acquisition and procurement.“(2)The Transportation Security Administration has only recently developed a multiyear technology investment plan, and has underutilized innovation opportunities within the private sector, including from small businesses.“(3)The Transportation Security Administration has faced challenges in meeting key performance requirements for several major acquisitions and procurements, resulting in reduced security effectiveness and wasted expenditures.”

Prior Amendments Not Affected, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–245, § 3(c)128 Stat. 2877Dec. 18, 2014“Nothing in this section [enacting this subchapter] may be construed to affect any amendment made by title XVI of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 [title XVI of , amending sections 114, 40119, 44935 and 46301 of Title 49, Transportation] as in effect before the date of enactment of this Act [].”Pub. L. 107–296Dec. 18, 2014

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 1 Administration
children.text: The term “Administration” means the Transportation Security Administration.

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 2 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Transportation Security Administration.

children.heading: Plan
children.label: 3 Plan
children.text: The term “Plan” means the strategic 5-year technology investment plan developed by the Administrator under .section 563 of this title

children.heading: Security-related technology
children.label: 4 Security-related technology
children.text: The term “security-related technology” means any technology that assists the Administration in the prevention of, or defense against, threats to United States transportation systems, including threats to people, property, and information.
